summary: This source is a BBC Radio 4 summary on healthy aging featuring multiple experts. Epidemiologist Professor Kay-Tee Khaw contributes specific dietary advice based on her research into the impact of diet on older people in Europe, emphasizing the avoidance of ultra-processed foods and the consumption of plant-based foods.

principles.statement: Avoid ultra-processed foods to minimize your intake of salt and sugars.
principles.rationale: Excess salt and sugars are predominantly found in processed foods, making their avoidance a simple way to improve dietary health.
principles.source_quote: [These] are mostly found in processed foods so avoid ultra-processed foods

principles.statement: Prioritize a diet high in plant foods, including fruits, vegetables, lentils, and nuts.
principles.rationale: A plant-rich diet is a core component of healthy aging and longevity.
principles.source_quote: Five servings of fruit and vegetables a day, if possible... high in lentils and nuts.

heuristics: Eat five servings of fruit and vegetables a day.
Avoid ultra-processed foods to cut out salt and sugar.

signature_quotes.text: [These] are mostly found in processed foods so avoid ultra-processed foods
signature_quotes.context: Advising on how to minimize salt and sugars in the diet.

signature_quotes.text: Five servings of fruit and vegetables a day, if possible

anti_patterns.why_it_fails: They are the primary source of excess salt and sugars, which negatively impact long-term health.
